reviews.review_title: Fun for a short bit of exploration
reviews.review_description:
Virtual travel is one thing I enjoy in VR. Going to some new environment, finding stuff, ... This experience provides a bit of that. It's a short experience where you explore a beach, follow a short list of tasks and collect some coins.

The graphics are decent, though suffer greatly from LOD and object popping (this also includes the coins not showing from a distance, which hinders their collections a little), as well as clear texture MIP map changes. Although the game has quite a few graphical options, they unfortunately don't address either of these.

It wasn't until the developer responded to my previous review that I realised that it's possible to get to a night scene. That transition wasn't clear to me. That scene is like the beach day scene, only at night, and has fewer things to do.

The game has 'difficulty levels', which change the number of coins that need to be collected. At the highest level there are 65 coins to collect. I only collected 62, but the game let me finish, saying that I didn't have enough money for a pudding.

For the $2 I paid, I feel that it was an okay experience, a little more than 3 stars, so I gave it 4. I don't think it's worth full price. I enjoyed The Dream Dimension more, as it has more environments and they're more imaginative. They also don't suffer from the distracting LOD popping. On the other hand, the tasks did add to the exploration. Overall I enjoyed it.

Wow! Simply stunning and one of the best experiences I've had with VR on any system, PC, Console or Mobile.

I love the way you can move freely to explore without any sickness and it's all so gorgeous to look at, listen to and relax in, just total immersion and escape. Cute little love story thing going on too, top marks and exactly what I was looking for but didn't even know it. Feels like the sort of game GearVR was made for. More like this please Oculus!

developer_response.body:
Update 1.2.0.0 is now live and features an optimised dynamic cloud system (new screenshots are coming) to enhance your sky gazing and overall immersion in the environments. The lighting has also been adjusted to minimise white-out from the sunshine while the default supersampling has now been increased for Galaxy S9 class and above devices to 1.8X making the visuals extraordinarily clear and sharp, possibly the best seen on any VR anywhere outside a top end PC rig.

Hello, could you please check out the new Hi Spec Reboot version (from 1.1.8.6) of the game and rate accordingly as the presentation is now significantly upgraded for all users and contains new and innovative features not seen anywhere else in all of mobile VR. Not only is the brand new and unique control scheme now complete allowing 6dof like immersion into the world but the annoying and uncomfortable framepacing issues that plagued the Oculus Go since launch are now solved along with a massive increase in the super sampling used across all versions leading to absolutely stunning visuals on the amazing Samsung and Oculus screens. A new in game graphics options panel rounds out your ability to enhance the stunning looks even further on more modern hardware (such as on later Samsung phones like the S9 and S10).

Update 1.1.5.7 is now live and further refines the Oculus Go controller movement bug fix as well as adding a host of advanced graphics options for Oculus Go users.

Thank you for your purchase and review.

I'm so glad you enjoyed it, please do play it on all difficulty levels to ensure full exploration, have pudding and swim at night to Beethoven!

Update with automatic game saving and other enhancements is now live!

reviews.review_description: Nice relaxing environment. Incredibly sharp graphics, clearest I've seen on the Go. Worth it just to experience that. Couple of issues, the buildings/shops are incredibly bright and completely white in places, which makes them uncomfortable to view and be around. Also, i enjoy watching clouds go past to relax but doing that with these just made me feel queasy. I think because the whole sky moves together it gives the feeling of the ground/player moving instead of individual clouds. Maybe there should be some realistic layering so that some clouds are fixed. Would enjoy some other relaxation features such as day/night cycle/option and rain. Looking forward to spending more time in it.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your updated review, I really appreciate the feedback. 

Update 1.2.0.0 is now live and features an optimised dynamic cloud system (new screenshots are coming) to enhance your sky gazing and overall immersion in the environments. The lighting has also been adjusted to minimise white-out from the sunshine while the default supersampling has now been increased for Galaxy S9 class and above devices to 1.8X making the visuals extraordinarily clear and sharp, possibly the best seen on any VR anywhere outside a top end PC rig.

reviews.review_description: Really enjoyed good for the money 👍😎

developer_response.body: I'm so glad you enjoyed it! 😎

reviews.review_description: There are no people. Maybe this is what the world will look like after the coronavirus wipes everyone out. Sort of like a rated "G" Leisure Suit Larry. After a tip from the developer, it was fun (the cursor turns green when you found the right item).  The environment is soothing and attractive. Love the tiki torches at night.
I can't figure out where to go. I swim for a long time but never get anywhere. I've only seen the 2 starting islands.

Update 1.1.5.7 is now live and further refines the Oculus Go controller movement bug fix as well as adding a host of advanced graphics options for Oculus Go users.

Update 1.1.5.5: New control scheme fix for affected Oculus Go controller users.

Thank you for your purchase and the review. 

As awesome as swimming around in VR is, from the start you need to go to the Ice Cream Stand at the centre of the first island and click it to get an ice cream and then go to the hammock to enjoy it (and continue to do likewise). Your wand and pointer will turn green to indicate exactly what to click on at any point in case of any doubt.

On the first island you'll get tips on how the game works; stand close by, snap turns etc.

I'm currently working on a Save Game feature which will go live when it's done and tested. (Edit: This is done and now live so automatic game saving is enabled).

Feel free to contact me at fb.me/VRGameStudio and let me know how you get on and thank you again.

reviews.review_description: Neat environment. Basically move about as directed by clues that tell you what’s next. Not the least bit difficult once u get the game is linear. Played in one setting—about an hour.
